theory.intro: A column graph shows category data as bars. A many-to-one scale lets one axis unit stand for several items.
facts: Each column's **height** shows the frequency of that category.
A **many-to-one** scale means one grid step represents more than one item (e.g. $1$ step $= 5$).
Bars are **equal width**, evenly spaced, with the axis starting at $0$.
steps: Choose a scale so the tallest bar fits with even steps.
Draw each bar to the correct height for its frequency.
Label the axes and each category.
